questions.subject: Geochemistry & Isotope Geology
questions.stem: In Goldschmidt's classification, elements that concentrate in metallic iron (e.g. Ni, Co, Au) are
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Siderophile = iron-loving; lithophile = silicate; chalcophile = sulphide; atmophile = gas.

**Working.**
Siderophile.

**Answer.** Siderophile

questions.subject: Geochemistry & Isotope Geology
questions.stem: The isotopic system best suited for dating very young (<50 ka) organic material is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Radiocarbon (5730 yr half-life) suits late-Quaternary organic carbon.

**Working.**
Radiocarbon.

**Answer.** $^{14}$C

questions.subject: Geochemistry & Isotope Geology
questions.stem: A mineral system has a half-life of $1250\,$Myr. After $2500\,$Myr, the fraction of parent remaining is _____.

questions.subject: Geochemistry & Isotope Geology
questions.stem: Which statements about rare-earth elements (REE) are correct?
options: A negative Eu anomaly suggests plagioclase fractionation
REE are normalised to chondrite for spider diagrams
LREE are more incompatible than HREE in most mantle minerals
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Eu anomalies track plagioclase; chondrite normalisation removes the odd–even effect; LREE are more incompatible; most REE are not radioactive.

**Working.**
Options 1, 2, 3.

**Answer.** REE behaviour

questions.subject: Geochemistry & Isotope Geology
questions.stem: A low initial $^{87}$Sr/$^{86}$Sr ratio (~0.703) in a basalt indicates
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Low initial Sr ratios reflect a depleted (low Rb/Sr) mantle source.

**Working.**
Mantle source.

**Answer.** Depleted mantle

questions.subject: Geochemistry & Isotope Geology
questions.stem: A basalt has initial $^{87}$Sr/$^{86}$Sr $=0.704$. Index its source (0 = mantle ≤0.7035, 1 = crustal >0.7035): _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Low initial ratios indicate a mantle source; high ratios indicate crustal contamination.

**Working.**
$$0.704\Rightarrow 1.$$

**Answer.** $1$

questions.subject: Geochemistry & Isotope Geology
questions.stem: A basalt has initial $^{87}$Sr/$^{86}$Sr $=0.7025$. Index its source (0 = mantle ≤0.7035, 1 = crustal >0.7035): _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Low initial ratios indicate a mantle source; high ratios indicate crustal contamination.

**Working.**
$$0.7025\Rightarrow 0.$$

**Answer.** $0$
